mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows or positive returns dominate during high-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real sales of autos and light trucks and housing starts indicate a synchronized consumer demand contraction, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 1.0% quarter-over-quarter during a supply-constrained environment generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days due to capacity utilization pricing power.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth yields negative or flat 20-day SPX returns during supply bottlenecks, with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% annualized reflects robust aggregate demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0% correlates with negative 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 0.4% triggers expectations of prolonged monetary tightening, suppressing S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 70% of high-inflation trigger months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Concurrent PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns with statistical significance at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in real auto sales exceeding 3.0% signals weakening consumer discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days during economic slowdowns.
failure_condition: Negative RSAFS changes during slowdowns are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 65% of cases.
